close_reason: Shipped in PR #16: process_data_packet and forward_link_request extract only needed fields instead of cloning full PathEntry
description:
process_data_packet (node.rs:1107) and forward_link_request (node.rs:1218) clone the entire
PathEntry struct to escape a borrow conflict with &mut self. PathEntry includes random_blobs:
Vec<[u8;10]> (up to 64 entries = 640 bytes) plus interface_name String — but downstream code
only needs (next_hop: [u8;16], interface_name, hops: u8).

Fix: Extract only the needed fields after path_table.get() instead of cloning the full entry.
E.g. let (next_hop, iface, hops) = match self.path_table.get(&dest) { ... };

This eliminates cloning random_blobs on every forwarded data packet. Combined with the
Arc<str> interface name work (harmony-b6p), the extraction becomes entirely Copy.

Files: crates/harmony-reticulum/src/node.rs:1107, 1218

Discovery: Zero-copy audit of harmony-reticulum during harmony-0p6.4 pub/sub review.
title: Avoid full PathEntry clone on data packet relay hot path

close_reason: Session state machine (sans-I/O) implemented and merged in PR #13
description:
Build a sans-I/O session coordinator (same pattern as harmony-reticulum's Node) that manages peer connections and resource ID mapping.

Scope:
- Session struct with SessionEvent (inbound) / SessionAction (outbound) enums
- Session lifecycle state machine: Init → Open → Active → Close
- Resource ID mapping: declare key expression ↔ ExprId for wire efficiency (avoids sending full string paths on every message)
- Peer tracking: connected peers with their declared interests and capabilities
- Keepalive timer: emit keepalive actions, detect stale peers
- Close handshake: graceful shutdown with undeclare propagation
- No actual I/O — caller drives the event loop (same as Node.handle_event())
- Tests for: handshake flow, resource ID allocation/deallocation, keepalive timeout, peer lifecycle, close handshake

Depends on: harmony-0p6.2
title: Session state machine (sans-I/O)

close_reason: Implemented in PR #14. PubSubRouter with subscription matching, PublisherId allocation, and full event/action cycle. All tests pass.
description:
Implement publisher/subscriber primitives within the Session, with key expression tree-based message routing.

Scope:
- Publisher declaration: register intent to publish on a key expression
- Subscriber declaration: register interest in a key expression (with wildcards)
- Push message dispatch: match incoming Push messages against subscriber KeTree, deliver to matching subscribers
- Outbound Push construction: serialize and emit SendMessage actions for remote subscribers
- Interest propagation: track which peers have subscribers for which key expressions; only forward where there is interest (write-side filtering)
- Local vs remote delivery: local subscribers get direct delivery, remote subscribers get outbound messages
- Tests for: declare/undeclare lifecycle, wildcard matching, multi-subscriber dispatch, interest-based filtering, message ordering

Depends on: harmony-0p6.3
title: Pub/Sub routing

close_reason: Liveliness tracking implemented and merged in PR #18
description:
Implement liveliness token presence tracking for online status detection without polling.

Scope:
- Token declaration: bind a liveliness token to a key expression (e.g., harmony/presence/server1/user42); token lifetime tied to session
- Token undeclaration: explicit removal or automatic on session close
- Liveliness subscriber: subscribe to token appears/disappears events on a key expression pattern
- Liveliness query: list currently alive tokens matching a pattern
- Timer-based expiry: detect tokens from disconnected peers via keepalive timeout
- Event emission: LivelinessAppeared / LivelinessDisappeared actions
- Tests for: token lifecycle, subscribe to wildcard patterns, disconnect detection, query current state, multiple tokens on same session

Depends on: harmony-0p6.3
title: Liveliness tracking

description: Phase 5 of harmony-content. Count-Min Sketch with 4-bit counters and configurable width. Periodic halving (counter decay). Window LRU (1% capacity). Main Segmented LRU (99% capacity). Admission challenge: frequency comparison between window evictee and main victim. ContentStore trait wrapping BlobStore with cache admission/eviction. Pin support exempting CIDs from eviction. ~12 tests: scan resistance, frequency-based admission, counter decay, pin exemption. See docs/plans/2026-03-02-content-addressing-design.md Section 5.

description: Define platform-abstract traits in harmony core that Ring 1+ will implement. Three traits: NetworkInterface (send/receive raw bytes), PersistentState (save/load node state across restarts), EntropySource (cryptographic randomness). These decouple the sans-I/O state machines from any specific platform, enabling the same protocol code to run on bare metal, microkernel, or full OS.
title: Ring 0: platform-abstract traits (NetworkInterface, PersistentState, EntropySource)

title: Client UX: flat navigation hierarchy with color banding

description:
Phase 2 continued: Link establishment is how two Reticulum nodes create an authenticated encrypted channel after discovering each other via announces.

Three-step handshake:
1. Link Request: initiator sends ECDH ephemeral + link_id to destination
2. Link Proof: destination proves identity, derives shared key, sends proof
3. Link Identify: initiator optionally reveals its identity to the destination

Implement as pure state machine (LinkState enum) with bytes-in/bytes-out transitions. Derive session keys via HKDF from the ECDH shared secret. Reuse harmony-crypto HKDF + harmony-identity ECDH.

This is required before any node can establish private channels — the prerequisite for "once two nodes discover each other, they take things off-band."
title: Implement Reticulum link establishment (request, proof, identify) as sans-I/O state machine

description: Allow WASM compute tasks to read immutable content from the content-addressed storage system by CID. When a WASM module emits a host call to read a CID, the runtime suspends the task (NeedsIO), resolves the CID via Tier 2 storage (local cache or network fetch), and resumes the task with the data. Since all content is immutable and auditable (CID = hash), there is no trust boundary to enforce for reads. This turns the NeedsIO path from an error into a working content resolution pipeline. Design notes in docs/plans/2026-03-04-tier3-compute-integration-design.md under 'Future: Content Read Access for WASM'.
title: Compute: WASM content read access via NeedsIO

description: Wire the Harmony node runtime into the unikernel event loop. The loop polls network interfaces, feeds events into the sans-I/O state machines, and executes resulting actions. Target milestone: two unikernel nodes in QEMU discover each other via Reticulum announces and exchange messages.
title: Ring 1: unikernel event loop — boot, announce, route, serve

title: Client UX: IntersectionObserver for visible thread tracking

description:
Phase 2 continued: The Interface trait is the abstraction between Reticulum's protocol logic and physical/logical transports.

Define:
- Interface trait: send(packet), receive() → packet, mtu(), online(), bitrate()
- InterfaceMode: Full, Gateway, AccessPoint, Roaming
- IFAC (authenticated interface) support: HMAC-based interface authentication
- Basic in-memory interface for testing (loopback)

This is the boundary where sans-I/O meets real I/O. The trait must be transport-agnostic so we can later plug in TCP, UDP, serial, LoRa, Zenoh, etc. Harmony nodes will use this to connect Reticulum control plane traffic across whatever physical links are available.

Key design: the interface reports its bitrate/capacity so the node can make intelligent decisions about bandwidth allocation — prioritizing control plane traffic and being a good neighbor on constrained links.
title: Implement Reticulum Interface trait and basic interface types

description: Float32 → binary quantization with MRL tier packing. SIMD-aware Hamming distance (XOR+POPCNT with scalar fallback). Benchmarks. Design: docs/plans/2026-03-07-harmony-semantic-index-design.md
title: HSI: binary quantization and Hamming distance

close_reason: Shipped in PR #23: ingest(), walk(), reassemble() with round-trip guarantee, 14 tests, structural sharing verified
description: Phase 4 of harmony-content. ingest(data) -> ContentId: chunk via FastCDC, hash, build bundle tree, return root CID. walk(root_cid, store) -> Iterator<Blob>: depth-first traversal yielding leaf blobs in order. reassemble(root_cid, store) -> Vec<u8>: walk + concatenate. Round-trip property: reassemble(ingest(data)) == data for all inputs. ~15 tests: small file (single blob), medium file (one bundle level), large file (two+ levels), empty data, exact 1MB boundary, structural sharing across versions. See docs/plans/2026-03-02-content-addressing-design.md Sections 2-3.
title: Merkle DAG builder and walker

description: Review suggestions: (1) frequency_based_admission test should assert cold item was rejected from LRU segments, not just that hot items survived. close_reason: Implemented in PR #19. ResourceSender/ResourceReceiver sans-I/O state machines with adaptive windowing, retransmission, cancellation, proof-of-delivery. 67 resource tests + Python interop tests. All review feedback addressed.
description:
Phase 2 continued: Resource transfer enables sending data larger than MTU (500 bytes) over established links. Chunks data, tracks progress, handles retransmission.

This is the mechanism that would power "Reticulum-as-last-resort" data transfer — the lowest QoS tier when no off-band channel is available. Implementation should include:
- Resource advertisement (RESOURCE_ADV context)
- Chunking with hashmap-based progress tracking
- Sender/receiver state machines
- Cancel support (both sides)

Lower priority than links and transport — those are needed for the control plane; resource transfer is for the fallback data path.

Depends on: link establishment (harmony-3aj)
title: Implement Reticulum resource transfer (chunked data over links)

close_reason: Shipped in PR #16: All interface name fields changed from String to Arc<str> across Node, PathEntry, and all entry/action types
description:
Interface names (String) are cloned extensively throughout Node routing paths:
- PathEntry.interface_name, ReverseTableEntry.received/outbound_interface,
  LinkTableEntry.outbound/received_interface, AnnounceTableEntry.source_interface
- ~15 clone sites in node.rs hot paths (per-packet relay, per-announce, per-link-data)
- Real nodes have 2-10 interfaces, so the same few strings get cloned into dozens of entries

Fix: Change all interface name fields from String to Arc<str>. All clones become refcount
bumps. Pervasive change across Node, PathEntry, ReverseTableEntry, LinkTableEntry,
AnnounceTableEntry, and the NodeAction enum variants.

Affected sites (node.rs): L786, L1003, L1029, L1131, L1132, L1193, L1201, L1255, L1257,
L1270, L1316, L1381, L1383, L1391, L1396, L1530, L1538

Files: node.rs, path_table.rs (PathEntry), plus action/entry structs

Discovery: Zero-copy audit of harmony-reticulum during harmony-0p6.4 pub/sub review.
title: Intern interface names as Arc<str> across Node state tables

description:
Phase 2 continued: Transport capability lets a Harmony node relay packets for others, making it a full Reticulum routing participant rather than just a leaf.

Core components:
- Path table: map destination_hash → next_hop + interface + hops + expires
- Announce propagation: rate-limited forwarding with hop increment, dedup via packet hash
- Packet relay: forward Type2 packets toward destination, increment hops, enforce TTL
- Transport identity: separate identity for the transport layer

A Harmony node should be CAPABLE of upgrading to transport when it makes sense (stable node, good connectivity) but defaults to leaf mode. This is the foundation for the "good neighbor" policy — we control how much transport work we volunteer for.

Depends on: link establishment (harmony-3aj)
title: Implement Reticulum transport node logic (path table, announce propagation, packet relay)

description:
Pure data structures for Reticulum routing knowledge and duplicate detection, no I/O dependencies.

**PathTable** — Maps destination_hash → PathEntry (next_hop, interface_name, hops, expires_at, announce_packet_hash, random_blobs for dedup). Supports insert, lookup, expiry culling, marking unresponsive.

**PacketHashlist** — Bounded set of packet hashes for duplicate detection. Circular buffer with configurable capacity (~1M entries in Python). Supports insert + contains check.

Both structures are pure state — no I/O, no timers, no interfaces. They get passed monotonic timestamps for expiry decisions.

Key constants from Python:
- Path TTL: 7 days (default), 1 day (AccessPoint), 6 hours (Roaming)
- Reverse table timeout: 8 minutes  
- Max path hops: 128 (PATHFINDER_M)
- Hashlist capacity: ~1M entries

Depends on: existing packet.rs, destination.rs
title: Implement path table and packet dedup data structures
Implement node coordinator state machine (leaf mode)

description:
Extends the node coordinator with active capabilities: registering local destinations, scheduling outbound announces, and routing outbound packets to the correct interface via path table lookup.

**Destination registry:**
- Register local destinations (name + identity) 
- Track announce schedule per destination (initial + periodic re-announce)
- Generate announce packets on schedule

**Outbound routing:**
- Look up destination_hash in path table → find interface + next_hop
- For single-hop paths: send directly on the receiving interface
- For unknown destinations: broadcast on all outgoing interfaces
- IFAC mask before sending (if interface has IFAC configured)

**Announce scheduling:**
- Initial announce on registration
- Periodic re-announce (configurable interval, default ~6 min in Python)
- Jitter to avoid thundering herd

This completes the 'leaf node' — a node that can advertise itself, discover others, and send/receive packets, but doesn't relay for third parties.

Depends on: harmony-b83.2 (node coordinator)
title: Implement destination management and outbound routing

description:
Upgrades the node from leaf mode to transport mode for announce handling. A transport node re-broadcasts announces it receives to help other nodes discover destinations.

**Announce propagation:**
- Forward received announces on all outgoing interfaces (except the one it arrived on)
- Increment hop count on forwarded announces
- Track in announce_table with retransmit timeout and retry count
- Stop rebroadcasting after hearing a downstream node rebroadcast (or after MAX_LOCAL_REBROADCASTS=2)

**Rate limiting (per-destination, per-interface):**
- announce_rate_table: track timestamps of recent announces per destination
- Enforce minimum interval between announces for same destination
- Rate violation tracking with temporary blocking
- Grace period for unknown destinations

**Dedup enhancements:**
- Random blob extraction from announce data (10 bytes) for replay detection
- Compare against path table's stored random_blobs list
- Packet hashlist cross-check

Depends on: harmony-b83.3 (destination management + outbound routing)
title: Implement transport node: announce propagation with rate limiting

description:
Completes transport mode with packet forwarding capabilities. A transport node can relay Type2 packets for destinations it knows routes to, acting as a hop in multi-hop paths.

**Transport identity:**
- Persistent Ed25519 identity for the transport layer
- Used as transport_id in Type2 packet headers to identify this node as a relay hop

**Packet relay (Type2 forwarding):**
- Inbound Type2 packets addressed to our transport_id: look up destination in path table, forward toward next hop
- Hop counting: increment hops, enforce TTL (max 128 hops)
- Header conversion: strip transport headers when delivering to final hop (Type2 → Type1)

**Reverse table:**
- Track received_interface → outbound_interface pairs for packet return routing
- Keyed by truncated packet hash
- Entries expire after 8 minutes (REVERSE_TIMEOUT)

**Link table routing:**
- Route link packets through transport hops
- Track link_id → next_hop + interface + remaining_hops

Together with harmony-b83.4, this completes the full transport node capability from harmony-b83.

Depends on: harmony-b83.4 (announce propagation)
title: Implement transport node: packet relay and transport identity

description:
Implement link table routing for transport nodes. When a LinkRequest packet arrives at a transport node, it should be tracked in a link table and forwarded toward the destination. LinkProof packets travel back via the link table (not reverse table). Once a link is established, link-associated data packets route through the link table for efficient forwarding.

**Link table:**
- Keyed by link_id (first 16 bytes of link request data = hash of initiator's X25519 pubkey)
- Stores: next_hop_transport_id, next_hop_interface, remaining_hops, received_interface, hops, destination_hash, validated flag, proof_timeout, timestamp
- Entries expire after LINK_TIMEOUT (~10 minutes for unvalidated, longer for validated)

**LinkRequest forwarding:**
- Transport node receives LinkRequest addressed to a destination in its path table
- Creates link table entry tracking the request
- Forwards request toward destination (Type2->Type1 at final hop, Type2->Type2 at intermediate)

**LinkProof routing:**
- When link proof arrives, look up link_id in link table
- Route proof back toward originator via received_interface
- Mark link as validated in link table

**Link data routing:**
- Once link is established (validated), data packets with Link destination type route via link table
- More efficient than path table lookup for established links

Depends on: harmony-b83.5 (packet relay + reverse table)
Completes: harmony-b83 (full transport node capability)
title: Implement transport node: link table routing for multi-hop link establishment

description: Implement VirtIO-net driver for QEMU/KVM and wire it to the NetworkInterface platform trait. Also implement PersistentState (in-memory or raw block) and EntropySource (RDRAND or VirtIO-RNG). After this, the unikernel can send and receive raw bytes over a virtual network.
title: Ring 1: VirtIO-net driver and platform trait impls for QEMU

description: Make the per-tick event processing strategy configurable. Currently hard-coded as drain-all router / drain-all storage / one compute slice. description:
Design and implement the Athenaeum — a compact content-addressed memory model for on-device use.

## Core Concept

Any 1MB blob can have a max-4KB metadata header ("athenaeum") attached that breaks it into up to 256 content-addressed 4KB chunks, each aligned on 64-byte cache lines.

## 32-bit Address Encoding (per chunk)

- ~18-20 bits: X (number of hash bits to use as address)
- 2 bits: strategy selector for address derivation:
  - 00: first X bits of chunk's SHA256
  - 01: last X bits of chunk's SHA256
  - 10: negate/invert chunk, then first X bits of SHA256
  - 11: negate/invert chunk, then last X bits of SHA256
- 2 bits: inline checksum
- 6 bits: chunk size in 64-byte lines (max 64 * 64 = 4096 = 4KB)
- 2 bits: recursive depth (supports 3 levels of nesting)

## Key Properties

- Collision-free by construction: the "strategy" bits give 4 derivation choices per chunk, enough to deterministically eliminate collisions
- Computing the metadata (finding collision-free strategy assignments) is a one-time cost; verification is trivial
- 1KB of metadata overhead per 1MB blob (256 chunks × 4 bytes)
- 64-byte line alignment matches common CPU cache line size, enabling efficient packing
- 32-bit addresses are right-sized for microprocessor/unikernel use (256-bit external addresses are overkill on-device)
- Integrates with Zenoh references — the rest of the system ships content to your process in the format you need

## Architectural Boundary

- Below NetworkInterface trait: location-addressed (physical DMA, hardware registers)
- Above NetworkInterface trait: content-addressed (athenaeum, Zenoh, CIDs)
- Ring 1 uses location-addressed heap for DMA; athenaeum is a Ring 2+ concern

## Status

Concept phase — exact bit layout TBD. Core idea is solid.
title: Athenaeum: 32-bit content-addressed chunk system for on-device memory

close_reason: Fernet AES-256-CBC+HMAC-SHA256 implemented, Reticulum-compatible token format
description: Reticulum-compatible Fernet: AES-256-CBC with PKCS7 padding, HMAC-SHA256 verification. Token format: [16B IV][ciphertext][32B HMAC]. No version byte or timestamp (stripped per Reticulum). Constant-time HMAC comparison.
title: Implement harmony-crypto: Fernet token encryption (AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA256)

close_reason: Already fixed: PacketHashlist uses [u8;32] since initial implementation
description:
PacketHashlist uses HashSet<Vec<u8>> but every insert is a [u8;32] converted via .to_vec(),
allocating 32 bytes on the heap for every single inbound packet (node.rs:919).

Fix: Change PacketHashlist to HashSet<[u8;32]>. One-line structural change, eliminates one
heap allocation per inbound packet with zero downside. [u8;32] is Hash+Eq and stack-allocated.

Files: crates/harmony-reticulum/src/packet_hashlist.rs, crates/harmony-reticulum/src/node.rs:919

Discovery: Zero-copy audit of harmony-reticulum during harmony-0p6.4 pub/sub review.
title: PacketHashlist: use [u8;32] instead of Vec<u8>

close_reason: Implemented in PR #20: harmony-content crate with ContentId, CidType, checksum, constructors, canonical vectors. 37 tests.
description: Phase 1 of harmony-content. Implement the 32-byte ContentId struct with #[repr(C, align(32))]: 28-byte truncated SHA-256 content hash, 20-bit payload size, 12-bit unary-encoded type tag + variable checksum. Type tag parsing via bit manipulation (LZCNT). Hash computation for blobs and bundles. Inline metadata CID construction/parsing. Depth extraction, checksum computation and verification. ~15 unit tests. New crate harmony-content depending only on harmony-crypto. See docs/plans/2026-03-02-content-addressing-design.md Section 1.
title: CID format: 32-byte ContentId struct with unary type tags

Declares sharded queryables on harmony/content/{prefix}/**, subscribes to harmony/content/transit/** for opportunistic caching, handles harmony/content/publish/** for explicit storage. Includes StorageBudget configuration and StorageMetrics for observability. Sans-I/O: returns actions for caller to execute.
See docs/plans/2026-03-03-node-trinity-design.md sections 2 and 4.

close_reason: Implemented in PR #17. Packet.data changed from Vec<u8> to Arc<[u8]> for zero-copy forwarding. All 409 workspace tests pass.
description:
Packet.data is Vec<u8>. Several forwarding paths clone the entire payload (up to 481 bytes)
to construct a new Packet with rewritten headers:
- route_link_proof (node.rs:1331) — per LRPROOF forward
- route_proof (node.rs:1521) — per proof relay
- process_announce_table (node.rs:780) — per announce retransmit
- send_on_interface broadcast (node.rs:631) — per interface × per broadcast

Fix: Change Packet.data from Vec<u8> to Arc<[u8]>. Forwarded packets share the original
buffer via refcount bump. NodeAction::SendOnInterface.raw could also become Arc<[u8]> so
broadcasts to N interfaces share one buffer.

This is the largest structural change in the zero-copy series. Consider whether bytes::Bytes
(standard in tokio/networking ecosystem, supports cheap subslicing) is more appropriate than
Arc<[u8]> given Harmony will eventually integrate with async runtimes.

Depends on: Consider doing harmony-j1d (PacketHashlist) and harmony-b6p (Arc<str>) first
as simpler wins.

Files: packet.rs (Packet struct), node.rs (forwarding paths, NodeAction), announce table

Discovery: Zero-copy audit of harmony-reticulum during harmony-0p6.4 pub/sub review.
title: Arc<[u8]> for Packet.data to enable zero-copy forwarding

title: Cross-verify Harmony identity/crypto with Reticulum test vectors

description:
AnnounceRateConfig has three Copy-able fields (u64, u32, u64) but only derives Clone.
This forces .clone() at node.rs:976 on every rate-checked announce.

Fix: Add #[derive(Copy)] to AnnounceRateConfig. One-word change.

Files: crates/harmony-reticulum/src/node.rs (struct definition ~L50)

Discovery: Zero-copy audit of harmony-reticulum during harmony-0p6.4 pub/sub review.
title: Trivial derive fixes: Copy for AnnounceRateConfig
